feature: PS1 parameter/command/arithmetic expansion at prompt render time (promptvars)
notes: PROMPTING says prompt string is decoded then expanded subject to promptvars.

feature: interactive Ctrl-C during foreground external command returns prompt and keeps shell alive
notes: SIGNALS describes SIGINT handling in interactive shells and interruptible wait behavior.

feature: each pipeline is tracked as one job entry
notes: Paragraph 2: shell associates a job with each pipeline and keeps a job table.

feature: background launch banner `[job] pid` is emitted for async jobs
notes: Paragraph 2: async start prints job number and PID of last process in pipeline.

feature: all processes in one pipeline share a process group/job
notes: Paragraph 2-3: pipeline members are one job and receive foreground-group signal behavior together.

feature: foreground process group receives keyboard-generated SIGINT
notes: Paragraph 3: foreground group receives terminal keyboard signals (e.g., SIGINT).

feature: background process read from controlling tty gets SIGTTIN stop
notes: Paragraph 3: background read from terminal is stopped by SIGTTIN unless caught.

feature: background process write with `stty tostop` gets SIGTTOU stop
notes: Paragraph 3: background write with tostop is stopped by SIGTTOU unless caught.

feature: interactive suspend char ^Z stops foreground job and returns prompt
notes: Paragraph 4: immediate suspend semantics including return of control to shell.

feature: delayed suspend char ^Y stops on terminal-read attempt
notes: Paragraph 4: delayed suspend semantics differ from immediate ^Z.
Paragraph 5: current/previous job references and bare `%` aliasing.

feature: jobspec name matching supports prefix `%name` and substring `%?text` with ambiguity errors
notes: Paragraph 5: prefix/substring resolution and multi-match error handling.

feature: `%jobspec` and `%jobspec &` command forms map to `fg` and `bg` behavior
notes: Paragraph 6: direct jobspec command forms are fg/bg shorthands.

feature: job status notifications are deferred to prompt by default and immediate with `set -b`
notes: Paragraph 7: default deferred notifications and notify-mode immediate reporting.
Paragraph 7: SIGCHLD trap firing frequency semantics.

feature: `exit` with stopped jobs warns once; second exit terminates stopped jobs
notes: Paragraph 8: warning-on-exit semantics and second-exit behavior.

feature: with job control enabled, `wait` returns on job state change
notes: Paragraph 9: non-`-f` wait returns on state change.

feature: `wait -f` waits for termination (not merely state change)
notes: Paragraph 9: `-f` termination-only wait semantics.
